message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Vicky's of Santa Fe (Indian Wells)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 457 reviews; Tripadvisor 293 reviews; Google approx. 571 reviews per third-party aggregator [UNVERIFIED — Google count not directly confirmed]
- Sentiment: mostly positive on atmosphere/music; mixed on food execution and service consistency
- Longevity: Operating since 1989 (~37 years); current owner identified in press as Marc Laliberte (2022-2026 sources). Earlier ownership chain [UNVERIFIED]
- Credentials: ["OpenTable Diners' Choice recognitions cited as 'Best Hot Spot', 'Best Live Music Venue', 'Most Vibrant Bar Scene' (frequency/exact years [UNVERIFIED])","Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice","Tripadvisor #5 of 25 restaurants in Indian Wells","Featured in Coachella Valley Weekly (2016) and Coast to Coast Newspaper (2022)"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces reliably for 'date night Indian Wells', 'best restaurants Indian Wells', and live-music dining queries in Coachella Valley. Notably ABSENT from Palm Springs Life's 'Where to Hear Live Music in the Coachella Valley' roundup despite being repeatedly OpenTable-recognized for live music — a meaningful gap. Does not surface for El Paseo / Palm Desert fine dining queries (correctly, since it's Indian Wells).
- Site structured data: Own site (vickysofsantafe.com) returned HTTP 403 to automated fetchers (bot protection / WAF), which itself is an agent-visibility negative — LLM crawlers and retrieval agents cannot read the canonical site. Site has clean human-readable IA (about, menus, reservations, live-music, show-supper-club, reviews pages) but schema.org markup presence could not be verified due to the 403. Reservations route through OpenTable (good third-party schema coverage).
- Visibility gap score: 7
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here are some of the best restaurants in Indian Wells for a date night with live music:

## Top Recommendations

**Vicky's of Santa Fe**
Vicky's of Santa Fe is perfect for date night, with its award-winning American cuisine and vibrant bar scene, with the lounge serving as a hub for live music and dancing. Entertainment in the Lounge Room features a live music program by desert legend John Stanley King with electrifying performances every night, two sets on Fridays & Saturdays. The restaurant is consistently awarded "Best Hot Spot" and "Best Live Music Venue" by OpenTable diners.

**The Nest**
The Nest is Indian Wells' most famous restaurant and lounge that has been serving fine guests, celebrities, golf and tennis legends since 1965, noted for its delicious food, wonderful atmosphere, great entertainment, and generous refreshments. At its heart, The Nest is a classic supper club — equal parts restaurant, lounge, piano bar, and dance floor.

**Other Options**

- **Lavender Bistro**: Lavender Bistro prides itself on providing superb live music nightly to set a warm and friendly atmosphere.

- **Adobe Grill**: Adobe Grill features live jazz trio Friday nights and live mariachi music Saturday through Thursday.

- **IW Live**: IW Live is the Indian Wells Resort Hotel's flagship dining spot with an Old Hollywood flavored atmosphere, open for breakfast, lunch, and dinner with a menu featuring American and Italian classics.

I've performed a web search for Vicky's of Santa Fe restaurant reviews in Indian Wells. Here's what I found:

## Overview

Vicky's of Santa Fe is an award-winning restaurant in Indian Wells noted for its outstanding American cuisine, established in 1989, and known for being a place for cocktails, dinner, live music, and nightlife.

## Menu & Cuisine

The menu features prime steaks, fresh seafood, rack of lamb, and pork chops paired with various sides such as classic twice-baked potato. Standout menu items include the "chicken pot pie" and "macadamia nut-crusted sea bass."

## Positive Reviews

Vicky's continues to offer outstanding food, warm service, and a welcoming ambiance—an ideal spot for a special night out. Year in and year out, service is amazing, the food is consistently delicious and the atmosphere very friendly. Vicky's of Santa Fe offers an exciting blend of "great live music," "delicious food," and "friendly service," making it a must-visit destination, with guests raving about the "amazing atmosphere" and "energetic vibe."

## Entertainment & Atmosphere

Entertainment abounds in the Lounge Room with a live music program by desert legend John Stanley King. The restaurant is lively, retro Santa Fe–styled and music-forward with jazz/cabaret vibes, where it can be loud in the lounge (great for dancing) while the back dining rooms offer calmer, conversational tables.

## Mixed Feedback

While generally well-reviewed, some guests have noted concerns. One reviewer mentioned the restaurant was very noisy and somewhat difficult to sit on chairs that slide awkwardly on the very slick tile floor.

## Practical Information

Reservations are recommended, particularly on nights with popular bands, and if you arrive without a reservation you may experience a wait.

Based on the web search results, here's what I found about Vicky's of Santa Fe and its owner Marc Laliberte:

## Key Information

Vicky's of Santa Fe has been in Indian Wells since 1989, and owner Marc Laliberte likes to say, "It's not just the place."

In 1989, Marc Laliberte describes the area on Highway 111 in Indian Wells as having had nothing there before. His father and uncle had opened The Nest in 1968, "and nothing in between."

## Restaurant Details

Vicky's of Santa Fe is an award-winning restaurant, noted for its outstanding American cuisine and has been consistently awarded "Best Hot Spot" and "Best Live Music Venue" by OpenTable.

The menu features prime steaks, fresh seafood, rack of lamb, and pork chops paired with various sides, including a classic twice-baked potato. A walk-in wine cellar features California wines from well-known winemakers to select boutique vintners.

Entertainment abounds in the Lounge Room with a live music program by desert legend John Stanley King, with electrifying performances with no cover charge every night of the week, with 2 sets on Fridays and Saturdays.

I found the Palm Springs Life article on where to hear live music in the Coachella Valley! Here are some of the venues and entertainment options highlighted:

## Restaurant & Bar Venues

The Nest features resident musicians Kevin Henry (Sundays and Mondays), Marisa Corvo (Tuesdays), and Bryan Magsayo (Wednesdays through Saturdays) who perform cover music made for dancing.

Local musicians perform most nights on the patio at a Mexican restaurant: Desert Suite (country, Mondays); Johnny Meza and Walle Larsson (all genres, Tuesdays); Lisa Lynn & the Broken Hallelujahs (Americana and rock, Wednesdays); and The Myx (rock, pop, and Latin, Fridays).

Big Rock is a venue where you'll hear raucous music from Fridays through Sundays.

A roadside dive bar offers friendly faces, juicy burgers, and on Wednesday nights, rock, country, and jazz sounds of multi-instrumentalist and singer Matt Jenkins.

Jesika von Rabbit performs regularly at Wildest Restaurant + Bar in Palm Desert.

## Key Points

While headliner acts for the Coachella and Stagecoach festivals are a major draw, the desert is also home to plenty of smaller venues with lively year-round music scenes.

You can access the full Palm Springs Life article directly at: https://www.palmspringslife.com/arts-culture/where-to-hear-live-music-in-the-coachella-valley/

Regarding **Vicky's of Santa Fe**, which appears to be the specific venue you're asking about:

Since 1989, Indian Wells' premier restaurant continues to be the place for cocktails, dinner, live music, and nightlife. Vicky's of Santa Fe is an award-winning restaurant, noted for its outstanding American cuisine and has been consistently awarded "Best Hot Spot" and "Best Live Music Venue" by OpenTable.

Entertainment abounds in the Lounge Room with a live music program overseen by desert legend John Stanley King. There are electrifying performances with no cover charge every night of the week. Additionally, Vicky's is also home to the Show Supper Club Series, an array of five Broadway-style dinner shows featuring Broadway-credited performers and music artists.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best fine dining restaurants in the Coachella Valley:

## Top Fine Dining Establishments

The Purple Room Supper Club in Palm Springs offers a unique blend of fine dining and live entertainment, known for its sophisticated ambiance and classic American cuisine with live shows.

Le Vallauris in Palm Springs is a French-inspired restaurant that offers a refined menu featuring dishes like foie gras and lobster bisque, with an outdoor dining area surrounded by lush greenery perfect for special occasions.

Copley's On Palm Canyon is housed in a historic property that was once Cary Grant's estate and offers a mix of American and European cuisine focusing on seasonal ingredients, with al fresco dining in a charming courtyard or cozy indoor setting.

Morgan's in the Desert at La Quinta Resort & Club offers a refined dining experience focusing on farm-to-table cuisine inspired by American Southwest flavors with local ingredients.

Chez Pierre Bistro in Palm Desert offers authentic French fine dining with an eclectic menu and extensive wine list.

Eddie V's Prime Seafood is a luxury dining destination known for seafood flown in from pristine waters around the world and premium hand-carved steaks, with artistically prepared dishes featuring seasonal seafood.

Morton's The Steakhouse in Palm Desert specializes in prime-aged beef and offers a classic steakhouse experience with an upscale atmosphere and impeccable service.

Melvyn's is located on the premises of the legendary Ingleside Estate hotel and is elegantly upbeat with authentic Hollywood glamour.

content.text: I have enough verification data. Let me finalize.

confirms: Yelp 457 reviews — confirmed directly from Yelp listing snippet (April 2026 update)
Tripadvisor 293 reviews — confirmed via direct Tripadvisor fetch
Tripadvisor ranked #5 of 25 restaurants in Indian Wells — confirmed via direct Tripadvisor fetch
Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice award — confirmed via direct Tripadvisor fetch
Operating since 1989 — confirmed in multiple independent sources including Palm Springs Life feature 'A Pearl of a Restaurant'
Owner Marc Laliberte — confirmed in Palm Springs Life feature and Coast to Coast Newspaper coverage; family ties (father/uncle opened The Nest in 1968) corroborate the owner identity
OpenTable recognitions for 'Best Hot Spot' and 'Best Live Music Venue' — repeatedly referenced across OpenTable and third-party coverage
Surfaces strongly for 'best restaurants Indian Wells date night live music' — Vicky's appears prominently in independent search alongside The Nest, Lavender Bistro, Adobe Grill

Does NOT surface for 'fine dining Coachella Valley' queries — confirmed; major roundups (Cavallo Ranch, OpenTable, Cathy Muldoon) omit Vicky's in favor of Le Vallauris, Morgan's, Copley's, Chez Pierre, etc.

Own site (vickysofsantafe.com) returns HTTP 403 to automated fetchers — independently reproduced; same 403 on Yelp and Palm Springs Life article URLs suggests broader WAF/bot blocking that also impacts LLM crawlers
contradicts: Claim that Vicky's is 'notably ABSENT from Palm Springs Life's Where to Hear Live Music in the Coachella Valley roundup' — PARTIALLY CONTRADICTED. The Palm Springs Life article URL itself returned 403, but a targeted site-search with the exact article title plus 'Vicky's' returned the article as the top hit, suggesting Vicky's may in fact be mentioned in the article body. The 'absence' claim is not safely verifiable from the outside and may be wrong; the gap-score rationale should be softened or re-checked manually.
flagged_as_unverified: Google review count ~571 — could not directly access Google's listing; third-party indicates a 4.4 average but no count confirmed
Exact OpenTable Diners' Choice years and categories (including 'Most Vibrant Bar Scene') — referenced in aggregate but specific year-by-year list not independently retrievable
Coachella Valley Weekly 2016 feature — not independently surfaced in search results
Coast to Coast Newspaper 2022 feature — surfaces in search results (ccnewspaper.com/2022/03 URL) but full attribution to Marc Laliberte interview content not deeply verified
Sentiment characterization ('mixed on food execution and service consistency') — Tripadvisor 3.8/5 rating supports the 'mixed' read, but no granular sentiment audit performed
Schema.org / JSON-LD markup on vickysofsantafe.com — cannot verify either way due to 403 (consistent with claim's own caveat)
Ownership chain prior to Marc Laliberte — flagged unverified in original claim and remains so
input.notes: Core review/credential/longevity claims hold up well under independent search — Yelp 457, Tripadvisor 293 and #5/25 with Travelers' Choice, 1989 founding, and Marc Laliberte ownership are all confirmed. The 403 on the own site reproduces independently and is a real agent-visibility negative. The most fragile claim is the 'notably absent from Palm Springs Life's live-music roundup' assertion underpinning the visibility-gap score of 7: a site-restricted search for that exact article title with 'Vicky's' returned the article as a hit, which suggests Vicky's may actually be present in the piece. The 403 prevented direct page-body confirmation, so I cannot prove inclusion, but I also cannot safely assert absence. Recommend manually re-checking the Palm Springs Life article before relying on the gap-score-7 narrative — if Vicky's is in fact listed, the convertibility framing weakens and the gap score should drop.
